title: Besleria aristeguitae
taxonomy: Besleria aristeguitae is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Besleria and is a type of epiphyte, which is a plant that grows on other plants, typically trees, without harming them. The classification of Besleria aristeguitae is further divided into its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiosperms), class (Lamiales), and order (Gesneriales). Besleria aristeguitae is native to South America. The classification of this plant is still a subject of ongoing research and study.

intro: Besleria aristeguitae is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the Aristeguitaea or Besleria. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Andean region, for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents responsible for its medicinal properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are found in the plant's leaves and flowers. Besleria aristeguitae is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal conditions.
